details: The "Manav Garima Yojna (NT DNT)" scheme is launched by the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Gujarat. It aims to enable nomadic and de-notified caste persons to live with dignity and become self-employed in small businesses and financially self-sufficient. Through this scheme, various business tool-kits are provided to these target beneficiaries so that they may start small enterprises and become economically independent.
benefits: The primary benefit is the provision of various business tool-kits to eligible beneficiaries so that they can engage in small business activities and become self-employed and financially self-sufficient.
The tool kits are given under the scheme targeted at nomadic and de-notified castes.
The annual income limit for eligibility is ₹6,00,000 (for the family) for most of the target groups.
Among the SEBC castes, the income limit is removed for Nomadic and De-notified Castes and backward castes.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must belong to the nomadic or de-notified caste.
The applicant’s family annual income should be ₹ 6,00,000/- or less in general.
In the case of Nomadic and Denotified Castes and backward castes among SEBC castes, the income limit has been removed.

online: The eligible applicant may visit the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal:
On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on New User ‘
Enter your Full Name as per Aadhaar Card, Gender, Date of Birth, Aadhaar Card Number, Email ID, Caste, etc. and then click on ‘Register’.
After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Now, click on ‘User Profile’ to update your profile.
Fill in all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Update’.
After updating the profile successfully, select the scheme appearing on the home page; an application form will open.
Fill in all the mandatory details in the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
Now agree with the terms & conditions and click on ‘Save Application’.

Submit the application form and take out the print of the application for future reference. The applicant can note down the application number to track the application status by logging into the portal using their User ID and Password.

faqs.question: What is the scheme about?
faqs.answer: The Manav Garima Yojna DCW (NT DNT) aims to help nomadic and de-notified caste persons start small businesses and become financially self-reliant by providing business tool-kits.

faqs.question: Who implements the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat.

faqs.question: What is the main objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The main objective is to promote self-employment among the nomadic and de-notified castes.

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Individuals from nomadic and de-notified castes can apply.

faqs.question: What is the type of assistance provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The assistance is in the form of “Sadhan Sahay,” meaning provision of tools or equipment for self-employment.

faqs.question: Where should the completed application be submitted?
faqs.answer: The completed application should be submitted on https://esamajkalyan.gov.in.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must submit their Aadhaar Card, Caste Certificate, Certificate of Professional Course, Identity Card, Income Certificate, and Ration Card.

faqs.question: How can applicants start the process of applying?
faqs.answer: Applicants should visit the e-SamajKalyan portal, download the application form, fill it, attach the required documents, and submit it online.
